sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Chelsea come into this match in strong form, having won four of their last five matches across competitions, including a dominant 5-1 victory over Ajax. Qarabağ FK, on the other hand, have had mixed results, with a win, a draw, and a loss in their recent outings, notably struggling against stronger opposition like Athletic Club (3-1 loss).

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Historical matchups heavily favor Chelsea, who have won both previous encounters against Qarabağ FK with an aggregate score of 10-0 (6-0 at home and 4-0 away in 2017). This suggests a significant disparity in quality between the two sides.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Chelsea's attacking prowess is evident with a higher expected goals (xG) output and clinical finishing, often scoring early as seen in their goal distribution (50% of goals between 16-30 minutes). Qarabağ FK show defensive solidity with decent tackling and interception stats (top ranks in league), but their low possession (45.3%) and poor final third entries indicate difficulty in creating chances against top teams.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Chelsea’s ability to dominate possession and create big chances (evident in recent matches) could exploit Qarabağ’s weaker aerial duels (41% win rate) and low conversion rate (33%). Qarabağ will rely on counter-attacks and set pieces, given their fast break stats, but face an uphill battle against Chelsea’s in-form players like Reece James and Jamie Gittens.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Chelsea’s superior form, historical dominance, and statistical edge, they are clear favorites to win, aligning with Sofascore’s 66% probability for an away victory. Qarabağ might struggle to score, while Chelsea’s attacking momentum suggests a comfortable win, though likely under 2.5 goals for the home side as per their recent trend.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Qarabağ FK leads Chelsea by a score of 2-1 in an intriguing matchup at Tofiq Bəhramov Stadium in Baku. Qarabağ has capitalized on their opportunities, scoring twice through Leandro Andrade and a penalty by Marko Janković, while Chelsea responded with a goal from Estêvão. Despite Chelsea's dominance in possession and territorial control, Qarabağ has been clinical in front of goal, setting up an exciting second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Chelsea controlled the majority of the ball in the first half and made significantly more entries into the final third and penalty area compared to Qarabağ. However, their inability to create big chances and convert possession into clear scoring opportunities has left them trailing. Qarabağ, on the other hand, has been efficient, creating big chances and capitalizing on them despite having less of the ball. Defensively, Qarabağ has held firm, winning more ground duels, while Chelsea has been stronger in the air.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Chelsea dominate the early attacking phases, pushing forward with consistent pressure in the opening minutes. Their persistence paid off with a goal, but the momentum shifted noticeably midway through the half as Qarabağ started to build dangerous spells of their own. Qarabağ’s attacking surges became more frequent and effective, leading to their two goals. Towards the end of the half, Chelsea attempted to regain control with a late push, but Qarabağ’s defense managed to hold firm during these attacks.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Qarabağ, Marko Janković has been pivotal, not only scoring from the penalty spot but also contributing defensively with tackles and interceptions. Leandro Andrade’s goal has also highlighted his threat in attack. On the Chelsea side, Estêvão stands out as their main attacking outlet after scoring, showing composure and danger in forward areas. Players like Andrey Santos, who provided an assist, will also be crucial for Chelsea if they are to turn the game around.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Chelsea push harder to level the scoreline, given their superior possession and territorial stats from the first half. They will need to be more clinical in front of goal and turn their final third entries into tangible chances. Qarabağ, meanwhile, may focus on defending their lead while looking to exploit counter-attacking opportunities, as they did effectively in the first half. The match remains finely balanced, and tactical adjustments from both managers could play a decisive role.
